cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. The question is asking what Carmen Rivera wrote the letter to do. The options are (A) order ice-creams, (B) promise to visit again, (C) express her thanks, or (D) give some suggestions. First, I need to read the letter again to understand its purpose. The letter starts with "Dear Disney," and Carmen talks about a recent visit to Disneyland with her family. She mentions an event that made their visit magical. The story is about her daughter Gloria who had an ice-cream that wasn't allowed on the ride, and a staff member named Murphy helped by holding the ice-cream and later getting a new one so Gloria felt it was the same. At the end of the letter, Carmen says, "We know that _ tried hard to make our visit special. Thanks so much for going above and beyond!" and signs it. The last sentence clearly shows gratitude towards Disney and the staff member Murphy for their effort.
